mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the monthly unemployment jump fails to predict subsequent equity drawdowns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in > 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in the CPI index accelerates market expectations for further rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI increases during tightening cycles fail to produce negative forward returns, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for actual Fed rate changes in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over a 20-day window following each data release.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters consistently yield negative or flat 20-day forward returns, or the historical correlation between GDP revisions and SPX direction falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed the consensus forecast by more than 150,000 jobs, it signals robust economic momentum that lifts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Strong payroll prints fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of instances, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where consumer credit outstanding expands rapidly while the federal funds rate remains elevated creates a liquidity-fueled demand surge that temporarily inflates equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence between rising RSAFS and high FEDFUNDS fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sustained decline in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Declining INDPRO during high inflation periods fails to predict negative forward returns, or the signal's predictive power reverses direction when inflation expectations decouple from realized CPI.
